ogTitle: Sunset Beach vs Swanbourne: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunset Beach and Swanbourne.
heading: Sunset Beach vs Swanbourne — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunset Beach, Western Australia and Swanbourne, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sunset Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Swanbourne (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunset Beach or Swanbourne safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunset Beach has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Swanbourne scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunset Beach scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Swanbourne scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunset Beach or Swanbourne?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sunset Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
